attempts.strategy: Milne Cor 1.5 via the GrpObj-induced hom-group: form phi = h / ((p≫f)*(q≫g)); show phi collapses the complete V-axis to 1 (= _hf of rigidity_lemma) and vanishes on the {v0}×W section; rigidity factors phi = q≫g'; section forces g'=1; div_eq_one.mp gives the decomposition.

attempts.lean_error: first attempt: `rewrite failed: Did not find an occurrence of the pattern ?X ◁ η ≫ μ in f / (f * 1) = 1` — mul_one/one_mul resolved to the MonObj monoid-object lemmas under `open MonObj`
attempts.insight: Under `open MonObj`, `mul_one`/`one_mul` are AMBIGUOUS and resolve to MonObj.mul_one (η ▷ X ≫ μ = …), the monoid-object identities, not the ordinary group identities.

attempts.strategy: Disambiguate the group identities with the _root_ prefix.
attempts.insight: _root_.mul_one / _root_.one_mul force the ordinary Group identities. Lemma landed sorry-free, axiom-clean {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}.

next_steps: Foundation for av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero (Cor 1.2) and the Prop 3.9 additive-defect-map argument. The GrpObj hom-group idiom (Hom.mul_def/comp_mul/comp_div + div_eq_one) is the reusable handle.

attempts.strategy: Milne Cor 1.2: apply Cor 1.5 to h := μ[A] ≫ α with V=W=A based at η[A]; both axis-restrictions reduce to α via monoid unit laws lift_comp_one_right/left; decomposition becomes μ[A]≫α = (α⊗ₘα)≫μ[B] (the mul_hom axiom); package as IsMonHom α (one_hom = pointed hyp hα).
attempts.code_tried: have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity (V:=A)(W:=A)(A:=B) η[A] η[A] (μ[A]≫α) (by rw[← Category.assoc, hbase, hα]); rw [show lift (𝟙 A)(toUnit A ≫ η[A])≫μ[A]≫α = α by ..., show lift (toUnit A ≫ η[A])(𝟙 A)≫μ[A]≫α = α by ...] at key; exact { one_hom := hα, mul_hom := by rw [key, Hom.mul_def, lift_fst_comp_snd_comp] }
attempts.insight: Carries THREE extra instance hyps on A⊗A ([GeometricallyIrreducible/LocallyOfFiniteType (A⊗A).hom], [IsReduced (A⊗A).left]) that Cor 1.5 needs with V=W=A — true for AVs but Mathlib does not auto-derive product instances. IsMonHom is the canonical, more-reusable conclusion. Axiom-clean.
